narration_blocks.text: At dusk, the road outside Larkspur Vale writes a new sentence across the earth. Cobblestones turn one by one toward the northern dark, though no road has ever gone that way. Mara Vey waits beside the impossible bend, luminous chalk bright on her fingers. “It knows your name,” she says, as the distant hills answer with a bell no hand is ringing. The first step is yours.

narration_blocks.text: A howl rolls off the marsh — long, low, wrong, bending the mist the way wind never does. Mara goes still over her charts. “Marsh Howler,” she says, naming an old fear precisely. “Reed-green, lean as hunger, eyes like fen-lights. They test courage before they test flesh.” She marks your map with two quick strokes. “The pack has found the ridge road. Decide what you are before they decide for you.”

narration_blocks.text: They come at dusk's edge — two lean shapes pouring out of the reeds, reed-green weed sleeked to wet hide, eyes burning like fen-lights over the drowned road. The howl that bent the mist all day now closes around you from two sides at once. Mara plants her staff; chalk light gutters along its grain. The pack has decided. The circle draws, and the first howler slides in to test your ground.

state_updates.chronicle_add: The Marsh Howler pack closed the ridge road.

npc_actions.action: Slides from the reeds, hackles high, testing your ground

entropy_use.purpose: Initiative — the Marsh Howler pack draws its speed

logs.sent: I trace whose hand signed the newest paper.

narration_blocks.text: The nearer howler feints and gives ground — an opening bought, not offered. Beyond it the second beast peels wide, cutting the angle to your blind side while its packmate holds your eye. Mara's voice comes low and even: “One breath. Take it.” The circle tightens — wet grass, bared teeth, fen-light eyes — and the strike is yours to make, now or not at all.

npc_actions.action: Circles wide to your blind side while its packmate holds your eye

logs.sent: Resolve Strike the circling howler.

narration_blocks.text: Your strike lands true. The howler under your blade folds into the wet grass, its fen-light eyes guttering out, and the second beast loses its nerve entirely — it breaks from the circle and pours back into the reeds like spilled water. Mara lowers her staff, chalk light fading along its grain. The ridge is quiet again, and the road north lies open where the pack had closed it.

narration_blocks.text: Brannoc hears it before anyone — a single howl, thin and hungry, rising off the drowned road behind you. One shape only this time: a stray of the broken pack, bolder for its loneliness, sliding along the reed-line with its eyes fixed on the smallest of you. Mara sweeps her charts under one arm. The stray means to test the weakest hand it can find. The circle it draws is small, and it is Brannoc's.

narration_blocks.text: The stray feints at Brannoc and meets the boat-hook instead of his throat. He holds it the way rivermen do, hooked end low, knuckles pale on the haft. “Mine to finish,” he grunts — the first claim he has made on anything since the crossing stones. The stray coils to spring again. His strike, his die: the table waits on Brannoc's own hand.

npc_actions.action: Coils low in the reed-shadow, waiting out the hook

logs.sent: Resolve Brannoc’s hook against the stray.

narration_blocks.text: The hook glances off wet hide, and the stray's teeth find Brannoc's forearm before Mara's staff cracks its skull sideways. It drops at his feet, fen-light guttering, and does not rise. The wound is shallow; the grey spreading around it is not nothing. Mara ties it off and names the grey without ceremony: marsh-tooth fever, the pack's parting gift.

narration_blocks.text: A howl rolls off the marsh — long, low, wrong, bending the mist the way wind never does. Mara goes still over her charts. “Marsh Howler,” she says, naming an old fear precisely. “Reed-green, lean as hunger, eyes like fen-lights. They test courage before they test flesh.” She marks your map with two quick strokes. “The pack has found the ridge road. Decide what you are before they decide for you.”

narration_blocks.text: They come at dusk's edge — two lean shapes pouring out of the reeds, reed-green weed sleeked to wet hide, eyes burning like fen-lights over the drowned road. The howl that bent the mist all day now closes around you from two sides at once. Mara plants her staff; chalk light gutters along its grain. The pack has decided. The circle draws, and the first howler slides in to test your ground.

narration_blocks.text: The nearer howler feints and gives ground — an opening bought, not offered. Beyond it the second beast peels wide, cutting the angle to your blind side while its packmate holds your eye. Mara's voice comes low and even: “One breath. Take it.” The circle tightens — wet grass, bared teeth, fen-light eyes — and the strike is yours to make, now or not at all.

narration_blocks.text: Your strike lands true. The howler under your blade folds into the wet grass, its fen-light eyes guttering out, and the second beast loses its nerve entirely — it breaks from the circle and pours back into the reeds like spilled water. Mara lowers her staff, chalk light fading along its grain. The ridge is quiet again, and the road north lies open where the pack had closed it.

narration_blocks.text: Brannoc hears it before anyone — a single howl, thin and hungry, rising off the drowned road behind you. One shape only this time: a stray of the broken pack, bolder for its loneliness, sliding along the reed-line with its eyes fixed on the smallest of you. Mara sweeps her charts under one arm. The stray means to test the weakest hand it can find. The circle it draws is small, and it is Brannoc's.

narration_blocks.text: The stray feints at Brannoc and meets the boat-hook instead of his throat. He holds it the way rivermen do, hooked end low, knuckles pale on the haft. “Mine to finish,” he grunts — the first claim he has made on anything since the crossing stones. The stray coils to spring again. His strike, his die: the table waits on Brannoc's own hand.

npc_actions.action: Coils low in the reed-shadow, waiting out the hook

narration_blocks.text: The hook glances off wet hide, and the stray's teeth find Brannoc's forearm before Mara's staff cracks its skull sideways. It drops at his feet, fen-light guttering, and does not rise. The wound is shallow; the grey spreading around it is not nothing. Mara ties it off and names the grey without ceremony: marsh-tooth fever, the pack's parting gift.

arc.title: The Cartographer’s Debt
arc.evil_plot: The Hollow Regent is binding living roads into a perfect imperial map, erasing every future that cannot be predicted.
arc.stakes: Home, memory, and the right to choose an unmapped life.
arc.style_bible: Woodcut-and-watercolor storybook: heavy ink outlines, muted earth pigments, candlelit interiors, weather always about to turn.

cast.name: Mara Vey
cast.visual: Silver-braided cartographer in an ink-blue coat, one brass lens over her left eye, hands stained with luminous chalk.
cast.voice: Low, dry alto with patient pauses and a smile hidden in the consonants.
cast.goal: Keep the old roads from vanishing.
cast.secret: She once mapped a road for the enemy.

cast.name: The Hollow Regent
cast.visual: Tall masked sovereign in layered black glass armor, a pale cloak drifting as though underwater, crown split into seven floating shards.
cast.voice: Measured velvet baritone, never raised, with a faint doubled echo.
cast.goal: Make every uncertain thing obey a single perfect map.
cast.secret: The Regent fears a place that cannot be named.

cast.name: Brannoc
cast.visual: Stocky young ferryman in a river-grey oilcloak, rope-burned hands, a boat-hook slung crosswise on his back.
cast.voice: Blunt river-cadence tenor, words spent like coin.
cast.goal: Get home to the ridge ferry before the season turns.
cast.secret: He watched the pack take the last ferryman and ran.
known_facts: Offscreen — wins quiet ground toward Get home to the ridge ferry before.

regions.name: Larkspur Vale
regions.visual: A green river vale of slate roofs, old orchards, white standing stones, and a northern ridge scarred by a road that should not exist.

regions.name: The Wayhouse Ridge
regions.visual: A wind-bent grass ridge stitched with dim gold lights, a shuttered wayhouse below with one warm window and three fresh tracks at the door.

bestiary.visual: A lean bog-wolf sheathed in reed-green weed, wet hide over hard ribs, eyes like fen-lights, a howl that bends the mist.

payload.player: I listen at the edges of the court’s smile.
payload.visiblePlayer: I listen at the edges of the court’s smile.

narration_blocks.text: You move, and the thorny road answers. The path presses toward the ridge. Mara chalks the gate onto her chart, watching the dewy horizon. Below, one wayhouse window runs toward you, and three ruddy tracks lead inside.

state_updates.chronicle_add: The ridge revealed a waiting light.

hero.name: Isolde Gallowglass
hero.background: A former road-warden who can hear when a path is lying.
hero.bearing: Weather-worn leathers, a longbow slung easy, and eyes that never stop reading the treeline.
hero.mark: a hairline of gold in one tooth
hero.keepsake: a bell clapper wrapped in wool
